reviews.review_text:
[h1] 8.5/10 [/h1]
Got the whole 3 games for a very cheap price on bundle sale. Solid game for its time, really fun fps story game. One of the things that I don't understand why they didn't improve is iron sights... they are still very bad but other than that story is good, gameplay loop is good, they removed some of the annoying stuff from the first game. Looking foward to play metro exodus.

Such a great game, a sequel to the first game! 
The atmosphere, the same as in the first story, was heavy, tense, and dark. The story was great, I liked how we got to understand Artyom’s story better and deeper. The game was not as scary as the first one, visually the lighting and textures were better than in the first game. It was still a linear game, but you can explore much more than in the first game. 
[spoiler] Also, I really loved the Little Dark One and would gladly sacrifice myself for him <3.[/spoiler]
 
I am really enjoying the Metro series right now, and I can't wait to play Metro Exodus. I definitely recommend giving this game a try if you already liked the first game!

Metro is all about atmosphere.
The shooting mechanics are... not amusing due to insane recoil and weird monster hitboxes. Characters often look like they are made out of plastic. The stealth levels could be a lot better.
Yet every second you play this game, every level you see and every weapon you hold makes you feel that this game was made with love, care and great respect. The adventure of Artyom are long and perilous, even more than before. But your friends will help you. Your kindness will be remembered and cherished like a rain in the desert.
In the end you will see the light of hope for the desolate, barren radioactive world.

The morality/karma system could work a lot better. Getting a SINGLE bad karma permanently locks you out of the good ending.

I'd know, seeing as I spared/knocked out literally every enemy I could, listened to every dialogue, donated to every beggar, fully explored (almost) every location, and got the bad ending. Because I stood in one specific spot before the bear fight. Literally simply standing in the wrong spot gave me a bad karma point!

Metro: Last Light was absolutely amazing in every aspect. Compared to Metro 2033, which is the previous game in the series, it improved significantly across the board. The graphics are stunning, the gameplay feels much smoother and more refined, and the story is simply fantastic.

Visually, the game is beautiful and incredibly immersive. The art direction, atmosphere, and environmental design make every location feel unique and memorable. The world is dark, detailed, and captivating from start to finish.

I highly recommend playing Metro: Last Light. However, make sure to play Metro 2033 first, since the story continues directly from the events of that game. After finishing Last Light, jump into Metro Exodus and enjoy the complete journey. It's an incredible trilogy that every fan of story-driven shooters should experience.
Killing communists and fascist in one package. I would recommend.
Every Metro game is a gem. The pacing speeds up at the end of the campaign, but overall it's rock solid. Well worth the price tag.

Pretty solid. Similar formatting to 2033, but it's a better story. The new enemies in this game are a bit interesting lol. Somewhat difficult at times, but I like these games a lot for the Survival Horror aspect. All of the games in this genre typically write a great story with decent combat.

The second best in the series of METRO TITLES!
- The gameplay is not that tough, but playing survivor mode on ranger difficulty is a bit challenging (PLAYING ON NORMAL IS FINE THO)

- The game is still buggy at some points (FORCES ME TO RESTART THE DLC LEVEL ESPECIALLY SNIPER TEAM)

- Stealth is total fun. You can literally get pass through the enemies nearly in front of them un-noticed in darkness.

- Story somehow relates to the previous title "Metro 2033" (SAME KEY POINTS TO D6, KHAN, THE DARK ONES, etc.)

- Good Ending is only possible when you collect enough Moral Points which sometime feels a bit awkward because you have to explore the whole map to hunt them, and counts on your GOOD & BAD DEEDS.

- Soundtrack is as cool as the previous title, but a bit more touchy.

- Interaction animations (Takedowns, Environmental) are still competing with the modern titles where the animations are completely missing in newer games (PLUS POINT).

- Also, please give ARTYOM some voice of freedom (HE INNOCENT IS TOTALLY A MUTE PROTAGONIST). I wish he could speak!

[MY OVERALL RATINGS] 9.5/10
100% RECOMMENDED if you have the played and enjoyed surviving in METRO 2033.

Honestly, Metro games are just too much fun. This one delivers the same awesome story and solid combat as the others. I used to play it a ton on Xbox before I switched over to PC. Definitely grab it and give it a shot.
The stealth is fun and the world and guns are sick

A worthy continuation of Artyom's adventures, it polishes the whole game, taking it to a technical evolution that maintains the essence and atmosphere of the first one. Without a doubt, it's a must-play if you liked the first one.

Its Metro 2033 improved, whats not to love?
It'll take a while before I move on to Exodus though, found myself too scared to move in so many levels...

Last level was kind of a buzzkill but other than that it was great, games goes on sale all the time so get it then

Pretty solid game, way better than Metro 2033 just because there are MORE interactions with people from seeing how they interact with each other or just the gunfights in general.

The story is great, no plot twists, but still a great plot. The main character is living with guilt, and there's an upcoming war for his new home, in a nutshell. The DLC levels do not contribute much to the main campaign, but it's nice to play as you go into different perspectives of people from factions of a Reich Heavy Shocktrooper or a Red Line Sniper (my favorite DLC), to Pavel and Anna (my least favorite DLC).

My only complaint is that I wish the game were more direct with the karma system, such as a karma level indicator. Oh, despite this being an old game, do not use Advanced PhysX, as my game constantly crashed as I tried to use Nvidia's optimization.
Metro: Last Light Redux has an incredible atmosphere, immersive storytelling, and tense survival gameplay. The dark world and detailed environments make every moment feel intense and memorable.

“A darker, more personal journey through the ruins of humanity.”

Metro: Last Light Redux builds on everything the first game established and delivers a more polished, more cinematic, and more emotionally driven experience. It doesn’t just continue Artyom’s story—it deepens it.

The atmosphere is still one of the strongest aspects. Moscow’s underground tunnels feel alive in a terrifying way, filled with tension, whispers of survivors, and constant reminders that humanity is hanging by a thread. When the game does step outside into ruined cityscapes, the contrast makes everything feel even more fragile and beautiful.

Combat feels smoother and more refined compared to Metro 2033. Weapons have better feedback, encounters feel more varied, and stealth is more viable if you prefer a quieter approach. The Redux version also improves performance and visuals, making the experience more immersive overall.

The story leans more heavily into emotional and moral themes. It’s less about pure survival and more about choices, consequences, and what it means to protect humanity in a collapsing world. Some moments genuinely stick with you long after you finish the game.

That said, it still carries some of the series’ limitations. AI can be inconsistent, level design is mostly linear, and certain sections feel a bit scripted. But these don’t really break the experience—they just reinforce its cinematic structure.

Pros:

Strong, emotional storytelling
Excellent atmosphere and world-building
Improved gameplay over Metro 2033
Great Redux visual and performance upgrades

Cons:

Linear progression
AI inconsistencies
Occasionally scripted feeling

Verdict:
A powerful sequel that refines rather than reinvents. If you enjoyed Metro 2033, this is an essential continuation of the journey.

Recommended 👍

Metro: Last Light is a masterclass in atmospheric storytelling. The game's post-apocalyptic Moscow is a hauntingly beautiful place, where radiation-scarred landscapes and abandoned buildings seem to whisper secrets of desperation and survival.
As you navigate the crumbling tunnels and forgotten stations of the metro, you'll be transported to a world of eerie silence, punctuated by the creaking of doors, hissing gas masks, and the ominous growls of mutants lurking in the shadows. The atmosphere is palpable, drawing you into Artyom's desperate quest for survival and redemption.
Since the announcement of Metro: 2039, I have been on a quest to complete and 100% all the Metro Games and all it's done is make me realize my love for the series. Metro: Last Light is a must-play for fans of survival horror. It's an immersive experience that will linger long after the credits roll.

Slight Spoilers for a bug:
[spoiler] For any of the other achievement hunters out there, there is a strange bug that happens around the level Regina, that makes the prompt to knock out enemies disappear. If you're trying to get the good ending, avoid any and all positive moral points for that level to avoid running into this bug. There are plenty of other moral points you can get that can offset missing them for the good ending. [/spoiler]

very atmospheric and immersive game
i got goosebumps in the ending
10/10 highly recommended

Melancholic, eerie and (almost) as great as 2033. The world of Last Light remains ever so compelling that you can't help but wander off to the broken building on the side and find out what happened there. It is an exciting sequel with all the charm and details retained.
My only tiniest gripe is with this story trope; it is that same overused, guilt trippy way of making the player feel bad for killing monsters in a world filled with them. That being said, I still love [spoiler]Baby Dark One[/spoiler] and would gladly die for him.

Awesome game, but the devs have abandoned it. I play on a 4k monitor, but the highest resolution it allows me to choose is 1680x1050. I've tried dozens of solutions and none of them work. This is a widespread issue among a lot of users. There is zero excuse for the devs to not have fixed this.

Do not buy, if you want to play at a reasonable resolution.

EDIT:

For anyone who comes across this review, I finally fixed it. I followed the guide below:

Borderless Fullscreen Windowed
Go to the configuration file(s) location.
Windows %LOCALAPPDATA%\4A Games\Metro Last Light\user.cfg
Steam %LOCALAPPDATA%\4A Games\Metro Last Light\<user-id>\user.cfg
Open user.cfg.
Change r_fullscreen on to r_fullscreen off.
Download Special K[github.com]
Copy SpecialK64.dll to the installation folder.
Rename SpecialK64.dll to dxgi.dll.
Start the game and exit.
Open dxgi.ini in the installation folder.
Set Borderless=true and Fullscreen=true under [Window.System].
Set OverrideRes=0x0 to the resolution of your screen (e.g. OverrideRes=1920x1080).

I got the guide from here:

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2221234910

HOWEVER, do not use the guide's Special K link because it is outdated (or, at least, it was when writing this). Instead, use this Special K link (make sure you get the latest release):

https://github.com/SpecialKO/SpecialK/releases

4A might be extremely talented artistically, but they suck as a business. First, they do the Epic exclusive BS with Exodus and now they aren't updating their games for modern hardware (for a series they are still trying to make money on). I love these games, and yet, I don't trust 4A at all.

Again, Last Light and all of the Metro games are amazing. But there is zero excuse for 4A to allow such widespread issues with their games because they haven't updated them for modern hardware/software. I mean, the guide I linked above was made almost 6 years ago as I type this...and they still haven't fixed it?...

Well, what can I say... Okay, I like this part more than the previous one, because I think it has more stealth (well, you can play it without stealth) and missing elements. Overall, it's okay...
 I recommend it.

This game is at least as good as Metro 2033 Redux. Visuals are still amazing and works perfectly on Linux.

9/10 - Would get radiation poisoning again.

100% completed.

Metro: Last Light improves on almost everything from Metro 2033. The gunplay and AI feel better (not great) and the story does a great job expanding the world of the Metro. The atmosphere is still incredible.

The DLC levels are really fun to play and you get to see the other side of things. The stealth is fun to play around but only on higher difficulty. Favorite game of the 3.

Highly recommended.
Great game with a very interesting and exceptional story.
This is my favorite version in Metro franchise. I think the environment looks good and you can sense the density of its atmosphere. I also quite like the missions in this one.

When I played Metro 2033 for the first time as a kid I was ignorant to it being based off a book series, so it was to my surprise that the game got a sequel despite the many issues that were brought up in reviews at the time, such as the stealth being lacking, performance being abysmal, along with a bunch of technical issues and glitches that marred the original experience heavily. 

Metro: Last Light redefined the experience from those critiques, making a game that at the time of it's release was infinitely well received, and for good reason. 

From the get-go the story picks-up exactly a year after the first, with Artyom struggling with nightmares caused by his actions in the previous game, additional friction is amidst as well since the Spartan Order has taken control of a high-yield supply bunker that was discovered near the end of 2033, D6 and it's supply cache are the subject of many ongoing conflicts in the Metro, and added to mix is the emergence of a surviving Dark One.

Throughout a good chunk of the story, especially early on, you may feel a little restricted, because despite the action packed intro, the game suffers a slow intro, with the introduction to the game mechanics, granted, if you were like me who played this game at launch right after the original, you could appreciate the updated changes to the stealth, with shadows now working effectively compared to the almost nonexistent function in the original, the newly added takedown system which further streamlines it, but there's a problem, if you played Metro 2033: Redux, you'd already be familiar with this improved system, granted this comes from someone who played the original, but in 2026, this slow start can definitely be grading. 

Other than that, Last Light excels at making you feel like the ultimate badass, with stealth in this game becoming my preferred way to play, and this comes from a guy who usually hates stealth, honestly my driving force for playing stealthy comes from the immediate gratification I get from non-lethal takedowns, I swear it's hilarious dog-walking a punk with a donkey punch to the cranium, like yeah, the lethal takedowns are quite bloody, but Artyom throwing hands never gets old. 

Weapon customization is also a thing that was added in Last Light, allowing you greater flexibility over how you play where in the original, you only had pre-determined weapons with their flaws, in this, you can effectively make a weapon that excels in every field, personally I found joy in using the Tikhar rifle, because reducing a heavily armed loser very existence to a steel ball and some air never gets old, eventually getting the Hellsing makes a great debate of which is better but all the other weapons feel just as good, with some new additions making the late game surprisingly more entertaining as you spend more time top-side. 

One of the biggest things to be admired about this game is that once you push through all the arbitrary hand-holding you have to go through, the game opens up and really trusts you to explore at your leisure, something that the original rarely gave, granted for a game series based on a book, I can understand the need in the original for limiting you in some capacity, something that was rectified in the Redux, but Last Light spreads it out more. 

I remember forming a sort of negative opinion based off someone else's saying the moments where you are free to you're own devices, that they are short lived, but if you really spend the time exploring every square inch you get a lot out of those sections, additionally you spend a lot of time on your own, so that entire opinion seems rather unrealistic given that there's plenty of sections where Artyom has no contact, even after the hand-holding you don't interact with your comrades for a great chunk of the early game, and even when you're all reunited you're separated immediately and this constant isolation is masterfully executed with the ambient tracks and music, and near constant reminder that whether your topside or in the Metro you're always going to be at odd's by yourself. 

At the end of the day, Metro: Last Light is a game that heavily expanded on a game that had it's fare share of problems, with a reception so great that 2033 got a facelift - featuring all the nice features mentioned, truthfully I didn't play through the other story chapters that were provided with this version, another great feature of this Redux version is that all the DLC is included, something I barely touched on. But overall Last Light is great and you should definitely play it if you enjoyed the first.

Awesome solo story driven experience

The theater was awesome to sit and watch 

I could see this game being absolutly top teir for its day

the Metro serious even if late till now is super fun and good , i loved this game from the very start to the end , definetly recommend , also I loved using throwing Knives and doing all the good moral thingys , turns out i had to knock people out instead of killing to get the good ending 
10/10 experiance would do that again and get the canon ending on the 2nd run lol

An absolute masterpiece of a sequel that improves on everything!

Metro: Last Light Redux is a phenomenal game and a perfect continuation of Artyom's story. If the first game introduced us to the terrifying world of the metro, Last Light completely expands on it with a more cinematic narrative, intense political drama between factions, and deeply emotional character moments.

The gameplay feels incredibly smooth and polished in the Redux version. The gunplay is punchy, and the stealth mechanics are vastly improved—using light and shadow to silently eliminate enemies or sneak past them entirely is extremely satisfying. The level design is a perfect mix of tight, terrifying tunnels filled with mutants and beautiful, haunting segments on the ruined surface of Moscow.

The attention to detail is still unmatched. Wiping mud from your gas mask, pumping your pneumatic weapon, and burning cobwebs with your lighter creates an unmatched level of immersion. The story keeps you hooked from start to finish, diving deep into themes of regret, humanity, and hope.

If you loved the first game, Last Light is a mandatory playthrough. It is a gripping, beautiful, and unforgettable atmospheric shooter.

Metro: Last Light Redux took everything I loved about Metro 2033 and made it even better. The gameplay feels smoother, the story is more emotional, and the world is more immersive than ever.

From intense firefights to terrifying moments in the darkness, the game constantly keeps you on edge. The atmosphere is absolutely unmatched — few games make you feel so connected to their world. Every station, every tunnel, and every surface mission feels alive and full of danger.

Artyom's journey is unforgettable, and the characters are far more developed than in the first game. The balance between action, stealth, horror, and storytelling is nearly perfect.

Metro: Last Light isn't just a sequel — it's an improvement in every possible way. It made me care about its world, its people, and every choice I made along the way.

A true masterpiece of storytelling and atmosphere.

11/10 — One of the greatest single-player experiences I've ever had. ☢️🔥
Atmosphere in this game is great  almost finest post apoclayptic environment ,story amazing overall great game

The game is too much about trying to find air filters and shining lights on mutants to kill them. It's not enjoyable. They needed to make a "God Mode" available so we could just walk through and explore and not dye, or make an Easy mode where you didn't have to struggle so much to kill mutants and enemies. This is a BS version of the original game and it's not worth purchasing. Don't waste your time.
guys we, yes WE as in the collective pronoun, HAVE to save D6. this game is so tuff and wonderful 10/10 recommend locking out and playing this who even really needs revision
great game,runs pretty good even on my old mac,gameplay story etc everythin is cool

great game i loved this one too the first was 10/10 this one was fs a 10/10 love the little dark one sadly got the bad ending but fs gonna go for 100% so gonna go for the good ending and i think that's the canon ending for metro exodus Im so exited for 2039 when it drops fs gonna be reading the book after playing this one fs my fav so far!

Metro: Last Light Redux builds on its predecessor strongly, feeling more spectacle and experience driven whereas the first game sometimes felt a bit gamey in comparison. I ran into few bugs and only had one hard crash near the end of the game where I had to verify my game files to actually get the game working again. My only frustration with this game is the frustrating lack of UX/UI at multiple different points of the game with soft locks, obscure instructions, directions, and senseless mechanics in boss battles that still feel quite gamey compared to the following game in the series; Metro: Exodus. All in all, it is still a fantastic game and I highly recommend it to anyone who likes the post-apocalyptic genre.

Great game. Finished the trilogy, would recommend!
English or Russian voice... i've done both, can't say one is better than the other.
Fantastic game. I've played the original Metro Last Light a couple of times. Especially now with the announcement of Metro 2039.

Last Light fixes some of 2033’s problems but introduces new ones. The linear level design gets old fast and the game holds your hand way too much. Had some genuine tense moments but they were few and far between. For a game set in such a rich universe, it feels surprisingly shallow.

Both games had so much potential that just wasn’t fully realized.

This feels like a stronger, more confident follow-up to the first game. It keeps the same dark Metro atmosphere, but the pacing is smoother, the action hits harder, and the story feels more personal from start to finish.

The world is still the best part. The tunnels, stations, ruined surface, factions, mutants, and little human moments all make the Metro feel like a place people are actually trying to survive in, not just a cool background for shooting. You can almost smell the bad air and poor life choices.

I also like that the gameplay gives you options. You can sneak through areas, use throwing knives, conserve ammo, or go louder when things fall apart. And they will fall apart, because this is Metro and apparently nobody in Moscow’s underground has ever had a simple Tuesday.

The Redux version makes it feel smoother and better-looking, while still keeping that heavy, cold, post-apocalyptic mood. Great atmosphere, strong story, better combat, and enough tension to make every gas mask filter feel like a personal financial decision.

Fantastic game. Two words are enough to describe this masterpiece of a game from a masterpiece of a franchise.  I don't really know why I decided to 100% this game, but I do not regret a second of doing so. I kinda feel like the original two games are overshadowed by Exodus and they shouldn't be. They're absolutely phenomenal, especially Last Light. Plot is amazing and gameplay doesn't feel so boring like the first Stalker games imo. Even if you don't like FPS, this game should be a must-play for you. Very strongly recommend!

Didn’t even wait to beat the game to make a review. PAVEL IS THE GOAT.

Edit: [spoiler] Deadass 1 minute after I made the review I was blindsided so hard by this game that the only comparable feeling to me right now is one of an old, happy, trusting, little dog being led around the back of the house by it's owner with promises of treats only to be shot point blank and left to die in the cold.[/spoiler]

An absolute masterpiece of atmospheric storytelling! 🚇☣️

If you loved the first game, this is a perfect continuation that improves on the original mechanics in every way. Highly recommended! 🎖️👍👍👍👍👍

I absolutely LOVED 2033, with that being said I didn't fully like 2033s second half. Though after beating Last Light I can say its perfect from the start to the end its an amazing game, much better than the first.

A worthy sequel. Usually I'm not one into horror games but Metro changed my perspective on trying out horror games. The story is quite interesting and the gameplay is cool and it has these small heartwarming interactions, which really makes Metro: Last Light standout as one of the best post-apocalytic games.

Can't wait to play Metro Exodus.
Its a little dated but cant recommend the series (specifically this game) enough

The game was pretty good, but the moral system works pretty weird. Why should i eavesdrop on other NPC's conversation to unlock the good ending? Like if I were talking to my friends, I wouldn't want another dude breathing down my neck while listening to our conversation.

Game was very fun to play older looking graphics but still looks as beautiful as ever and runs really smooth has wonderfull gameplay and it really has a story to tell even more better than the first game 11/10 i do recommend
absolute banger of the game. i just love metro franchise and last light didn't fail me at all. a worthy sequel to 2033 and i am glad they didn't mess it up.

This is a significant improvement over Metro 2033 Redux. While the engine is the same, the story is better. The missions have more enemies, and the enemies are more dangerous. I still would not say it's a challenge to the player on the whole, but hard enough to at least not be boring. Given the games age, it's worthwhile if found on sale.

opa comrade vodka </3

9/10 

the bad ending and the ost was one of the best endings i ever see
i loved pavel and his personality
the story and artyom notes is piece of art 
his thoughts about evil and heroism 
i loved this game and cant wait to try out metro exodus

[h1]Metro: Covid & Babysitting[/h1]

[h2]The Good[/h2]
[list]
    [*]Everything that was good in the first game, is the same in this one, satisfying gunplay, fantastic atmosphere and many others
    [*]One of my bigger icks with the first game has been solved in this game, monster encounters and human encounters are a lot more balanced in this game, instead of mostly humans. It was a joy blasting through the different monsters, including the [spoiler]imposing boss designs[/spoiler]
    [*]While not much in gameplay has changed, the new [spoiler]snipers[/spoiler] introduced are incredibly satisfying to play with
    [*]The [spoiler]Dark One child wearing clothes[/spoiler] is super wholesome lol
    [*]The DLC/extra missions are really fun, they either add unique gameplay mechanics, or add extra perspectives to the story, I especially love Kshatriya & Anna
[/list]

[h2]The Bad[/h2]
[list]
    [*]This game is a SLOG to replay, there's a lot more unskippable dialogue and walking sequences compared to the first game, much longer as well. Same applies to Moral Points, a lot of long conversations you have to listen to, and doesn't help that you potentially need to listen to them more than once if you need to reload a save
    [*]The [spoiler]Dark One child having the voice of a generic child[/spoiler] really throws me off a bit, I understand it's probs a budget thing, but still
[/list]

[h2]The Random[/h2]
[list]
    [*]My boi Artyom [spoiler]got some GAME[/spoiler], HELL YEAH
    [*]Stealth is pretty much the same with the first game, but this time around there's a lot more instances where you need to do stealth for Achievements or Moral Points, but it still works well for what it is (I still prefer guns blazing gunfights, esp with the snipers)
    [*]It's honestly the same game as 2033 gameplay wise lmao, like almost no new mechanics, and a lot of things are just the exact same
[/list]

[h2]Overall Thoughts[/h2]
Honestly this is almost the same game as 2033 gameplay wise, so if you liked the first game, you'll like this one. Personally liked this one more with more monster encounters, guns and enemy types!

I spent 10 minutes sitting by a campfire in a fictional subway station just listening to a guy play the guitar while a mutant pack howled outside. 

This game, and the entire series to follow, is a certified masterpiece. 10/10, would wipe blood off my gas mask again.

I killed the giant shrimp in the swamp arc, after that they were supposed to throw the log so that I can reach the church, but they never did. The timer of the mask ran out and I died. I tried restarting the game and this keeps on happening to me. I cannot progress due to this glitch. Its just sad.

ABSOLUTE MASTERPIECE

I'm surprised the sequel somehow manages to be even better than the already amazing first entry!
Everything, mechanics, musics etc feel just like in the first, but with minor improvements everywhere, I just felt like playing the first game and continuing the adventure!

Crazy how the game is so diversified in gameplay, and environments are insanely well detailed and polished. Really feels like playing a Half Life game, and this was over TEN YEARS AGO.
God bless the devs, can't wait for Exodus and 2039!

it runs perfect, occasional frame drops, but only to about 40fps. great gunplay, really fun combat, amazing soundtrack, great story, amazing voice acting. This game makes me want to buy the Metro books by Dmitry Glukhovsky.

pyrocynical should do a video on this game.

Literally the same game as 2033 with no improvements so I'll just copy my review, could have been one big 30h game.

"A story driven FPS that is short and on point instead of drag out 30h campaign with good world building and lores. Shame the rudimentary gameplay is holding the game back from being a 8/10 to a 7/10 had it been the same quality as let's say Resident Evil Remakes, maybe it's time for remake?"
There are some small story DLCs and combat DLCs, I do not like the wave survival arena one, the game mechanics aren't good enough to have combat focus DLCs
Fun game, can't complete final mission due to it continuously crashing when it loads. otherwise pretty fun
game looks better than any modern game and it runs without frying your pc STORY IS AMAZING, GAMEPLAY AMAZING AND EVEN THE ACHIEVEMENTS ARE AMAZING

I'm not saying the previous one (2033) is bad, but if you don't try this one because the previous one was not that good, you're missing a lot of fun.
They both look the same but this one is better, more depth in the story and better combat, fighting monsters is good but fighting people is not as good as in this one, played both on ranger hardcore and this one is great, people are smarter and tougher than the previous one

The game felt a lot less grounded and survival horror than the last one. The story was ok but the heavy focus on the factions of the metro instead of the characters and the world made in less interesting to me this time around. The gameplay was pretty much the same as the previous one, but the level and mission design was worse imo, if you want the good ending the game just has you stading around listening to generic dialogue or trying to sneak around when the stealth systems are super buggy. Obviously a game of its time but i remembered it being better, so hasnt aged the best for me.

Not bad, but just above average in my opinion.

Another great Metro game. Last Light expands everything that made the first one special—immersive atmosphere, tense gameplay, and a thoughtful story that stays with you. That perfect Slavic doomer vibe is still there.[b] Definitely recommended. [/b]

worthy successor to the first game, am looking forward to playing the next one. 
runs perfectly on linux using proton.
Pretty good game overall. Smooth and clean. My only problem is there is no NG+ :(

7.2 out of 10 

Pros:
-Good Graphics
-Good Story and voice acting
-Fun shooter

Cons:
-Still feels like the gun restrictions are too heavy you should be able to have more than 3 guns at a time
- Storyline was a little cheesy and weird

I picked up the Metro series on sale when I was getting burned out with big open world games, looking for shorter, more of a succinct story. I enjoyed the Metro series for the most part, does not over stay it's welcome. It can be frustrating at times when you clearly are supposed to go a certain way but it is not clear- had to look up some things- one section in particular you are supposed to go into an air duct that I would never had guessed, there is a lot of stuff in the background and is not always obvious what is a barrier and what is real path.
Venice in Metro is proof that even after nuclear apocalypse, humans still will find way to open stripclubs and bars.
It may have some slight issues running on modern / current gen hardware but this game is a Classic, its story holds up even to today cannot recommend it highly enough.

If you played the first Metro game, and enjoyed it, Last Light offers functionally more of the same. Sure there are a few new guns, you go to different places, but the gameplay is the same.

What I find insufferable is the constant, finger-wagging, browbeating moralizing. "What? You don't want to forgive this genuinely evil guy who slaughtered a whole metro for a test? You monster!" "What do you mean you did the canon ending of the Book that this game series is based on? You should feel guilty!" I cannot say more without story spoilers, but it is a chain of infuriating lectures from the word 'Go.'
Continued the series right after playing Metro 2033 Redux. More of the same, enjoyed every minute of it, would recommend.
Pretty good follow up to 2033. Improved in pretty much every aspect. Story is still kinda mid but game is still dope. Would get stuck in an apocalyptic metro again. 8.4/10
great game. only real complaint is that there is a significant drop in the quality of writing compared to 2033.

The game keeps crashing for some odd reason it was running very well when I first bought it now it's crashing whenever I load into the game I have refreshed it multiple times and the problem still persists I wonder if this is happening to anyone else or it's just me.
I am not into FPS too much but I love this game and the rest in the series. They are beautiful, have an interesting story, and are very enjoyable overall.
like i commented on the first game its worth buying all 3 with the sales pack and worth 100% all 3 of them

I really recommend it. You can play stealthily in most levels, which is a big plus for me. A lot of people say that getting the good karma points is quite tedious, but if you go for a stealthier run and try to explore as much as you can, in my opinion, it’s not that hard. The story is also very good and immersive. Probably my favourite Metro game.

Great game again, often on sale, grabbed it for like 2 dollars. It is a continuation of the story and great example that with gameplay and story devs dont have to change anything, ui is the same, guns, other assets, and its great. Few battles were hard, but overall I am starting to like this type of short story games.

ok so gameplay wise amazing game love the story and the moral points system as a whole BUT this is big running the steam verson has a pit of bugs amd requires some set up but running it in adm,inistor help eleviate 90% of the bugs
Nice Story, Character and controls feel natural. Play the first game before playing this one.

Not as good as the first game, but still very cool. The only thing I didn't like was the moral point ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ -- there is no reason why the ending of the game should suck for me because I killed people who tried to kill me. Pseudomorality. It's not like I was ravaging innocents.

Perfect. So f***ing immersive. Only even 'BALSHOI' scenes are 10/10. Easily Masterpiece!

Such a good Second game to the series. I played this game back when it came out and just picked it up again on sale. Worth the money and a great story. Its an old game so glitches do happen but worth playing.

My one and only book and game series true love. Played it through and through like 7 times (before Steam) and never ever did it disapoint or feel old. I can´t recommend it any more than 100/10. Same with 2033

Metro: Last Light Redux is a post-apocalyptic game with a dark atmosphere and a very immersive story. The game takes players into the underground world of the Moscow Metro after a nuclear war, where humans struggle to survive against mutants and conflicts between factions.

The story in Metro: Last Light Redux is very interesting because it not only focuses on action, but also on morality, survival, and the emotional journey of the main character, Artyom. The atmosphere feels tense and sometimes scary, especially when exploring dark tunnels filled with mutants and limited resources.

From the gameplay side, the combat is satisfying and realistic. Ammunition is limited, so players cannot just shoot carelessly and must think carefully while exploring or fighting enemies. Stealth mechanics are also very good and make the gameplay more varied. The graphics in the Redux version are a big improvement compared to the original game, with better lighting and environmental details that make the world feel more alive.

One of the best parts of the game is its immersion. The sound design, soundtrack, and atmosphere make players truly feel like they are surviving in a ruined world. Overall, Metro: Last Light Redux is highly recommended for players who enjoy story-driven games, survival gameplay, and post-apocalyptic settings.
like the previous titlel it currently hard crashes every few minutes that require PC shutdowns. I would not buy this game in this current state despite it being a good game.

Good game. I enjoyed it much better than the first one and had way less. issues with the game it ran smoothly throughout my whole experience. I would recommend. Playing through it if you've never played the Metro series. 7/10
great game, a lot like first one, but less buggy and more fun

9/10 game for sure! Not sure how this wasn't on my radar at all over the years. For a 2014 game, this was extremely well done and polished. I had some of my most freightning moments in gaming from this game. If you have phobias, they are most likely going to prove it at some point!

Only thing holding it back from a 10/10 imo was the "final fight". Cool thematically, but didn't feel as satisfying as the build up would have you believe. Also there were some levels that could have just been left out and the experience would have most likely increased for it; some walkathons or boring stealth levels. Personally the story was a little confusing at times, but I think for the most part it was well done.

If you've played Metro 2033, then you already know what you're getting into. If you haven't, then go do that first for this is a proper sequel in all aspects. This game does what Metro 2033 already did well, and makes it even better.
The atmosphere, sound design was already amazing, but this time I felt the story was a little more tied together. Multiple endings, each choice you make has a consequence. I liked that. I personally got the terrible ending, but play like a 'good' person and you might just get the better ending. 
Still, the story wasn't anything to rave about, but as an experience, pairing it with the impeccable atmosphere and tension building which this game is amazing at doing already, the story holds up, a little better than 2033 in my opinion. 
Having played exodus for a few hours now, I know for a fact that the devs are listening to feedback, and they are doing something right. They just need someone to write better. The story can be made a lot more engaging if they want to. 6.5/10

Having just finished my first playthrough of all three Metro games, I can highly recommend all of them.  Grab 'em all in a bundle deal and play them in order.  Only thing I'll add is all of them played near perfectly on Linux with the latest Proton.  Smooth, high FPS and the Redux/PC Enhanced versions look great, even by 2026 standards.

You know it’s a good game when you start playing, zone out, and then come to seven hours later hungry, thirsty, and with a headache — and when you stand up, your legs feel like pool noodles. And… this game has boobs 👍

I play the game many hours and still enjoy every run. The graphics look very beautiful, the dark tunnels and ruined Moscow have very deep atmosphere and light effects are excellent. The textures and shadows make the world feel alive and scary. Gameplay is smooth for me, lighting and weather make each level feel different, and the sound design add so much tension. Even on my mid-range PC the game runs well and still look good, so I think this edition bring big value for players who want single-player immersion.

The story is strong and I like the characters, it feels mature and sometimes sad, with many choices that make you think. Achievements are complex and rewarding, some require stealth, some need very hard choices, so I feel proud when finish them. The game balance of shooting and stealth is good, and the survival parts keep you careful with ammo and filters. If you like story-driven shooter with great visuals and challenging achievements, Metro: Last Light Redux is very recommended to buy on Steam.

A great sequel, even more content added.

Weapons are nice to use, graphics and sounds are great, atmosphere is fantastic.

Highly recommended!

Disclaimer : It did crash on me a couple of times, no more than that, reason(s) unknown to me, but after restarting it played just fine again for a long while. No issues whatsoever, minor ding.

This game is a masterpiece and so worth your time.
Audio, visuals, gameplay, mechanics, story - all of it.
Thoroughly enjoyed every second of my playthrough.

It feels proper, decently fleshed out.
I loved it and will surely be playing other installments.
I can warmly recommend you give it a try.

A Journey Through Ruins and Humanity 
 
Why *Metro: Last Light Redux* Still Deserves Your Time

There are games that entertain you for a weekend, games that impress you with graphics for a few hours, and then there are games that stay with you long after the credits roll. Metro: Last Light Redux belongs firmly in that last category. Even years after release, it remains one of the most immersive, atmospheric, and emotionally engaging first-person shooters I have ever played. This is not just a game about shooting mutants in dark tunnels — it is a story about survival, fear, morality, hope, and what remains of humanity after the world has collapsed.

I went into the game expecting a gritty post-apocalyptic shooter. What I got instead was an unforgettable experience filled with tension, emotional moments, incredible world-building, and some of the best atmosphere the gaming industry has ever produced. If you enjoy games that make you feel truly immersed in their world, this is a masterpiece worth experiencing.

The setting alone deserves massive praise. The Moscow Metro system after nuclear war feels terrifyingly believable. Every station has its own culture, politics, economy, and struggles. Some stations are crowded and relatively safe, filled with traders, children, soldiers, and survivors trying to maintain a normal life. Others are dark death traps where every shadow feels dangerous. The game constantly reminds you that humanity is barely hanging on. Bullets are currency, filters are life support, and every journey into the tunnels feels like a risk.

What makes the world so memorable is the attention to detail. NPCs talk about daily problems, fears, rumors, and hopes. You can overhear conversations that have nothing to do with the main story, but they make the Metro feel alive. Small details like flickering lights, distant screams, radio chatter, dripping water, and the sound of creatures moving in the dark create an atmosphere that few games can match. Even when nothing is happening, the game keeps you tense.

The graphics in the Redux version still hold up surprisingly well today. The lighting system is especially impressive. Darkness in this game is not just visual decoration — it becomes part of the gameplay itself. Your flashlight cutting through black tunnels while your gas mask fogs up creates moments of genuine anxiety. Sometimes I found myself moving slowly just because I was afraid of what might appear in front of me. The environmental design deserves huge credit because every location feels handcrafted with purpose.

The sound design is equally phenomenal. Wearing headphones while playing this game completely changes the experience. You hear distant growls, footsteps echoing through tunnels, debris shifting, mutant noises in the background, and whispers in abandoned areas. The game understands how to use silence effectively too. Some of the scariest moments happen when everything suddenly becomes quiet. The soundtrack itself is subtle but emotional, supporting the tone perfectly without overpowering the experience.

Gameplay-wise, the shooting feels satisfying and grounded. Weapons look improvised and worn down, fitting the setting perfectly. Unlike many modern shooters where guns feel overly polished or unrealistic, the weapons here feel heavy, dangerous, and valuable. Every bullet matters, especially on higher difficulties. Resources are limited enough that you cannot simply spray ammo carelessly through every encounter. This creates a survival-focused style of gameplay where preparation and accuracy are important.

One thing I really appreciated was the freedom in how you approach situations. Many levels allow stealth, direct combat, or a mixture of both. Sneaking through enemy territory while quietly taking out lights and avoiding detection can feel incredibly rewarding. The stealth mechanics are simple but effective. Watching guards react naturally to missing allies or broken lights adds tension to infiltration sections. At the same time, if things go wrong, combat becomes chaotic and intense very quickly.

The human enemies are interesting because they often feel more dangerous than the mutants. Different factions within the Metro are constantly fighting for power, and the political side of the world adds depth to the story. The game does a good job showing that the apocalypse did not magically unite humanity. Fear, greed, extremism, and desperation still exist everywhere. Some factions believe in order through control, others through violence, and others simply try to survive without becoming monsters themselves.

Speaking of monsters, the mutant designs are genuinely disturbing. They are not just generic zombies or random creatures. Each type behaves differently and creates unique tension. Some attack aggressively in packs while others stalk you slowly. The Librarians remain some of the most intimidating enemies I have ever encountered in gaming. Encounters with them are unforgettable because the game forces you to stay calm under pressure instead of simply shooting everything immediately.

The story deserves special recognition because it balances personal emotion with larger themes about humanity and survival. Artyom is not the loud, overly charismatic action hero common in many shooters. Instead, he feels like a believable survivor trying to navigate impossible situations. His journey through the Metro becomes increasingly emotional as the game explores trust, revenge, fear, sacrifice, and redemption.

Without spoiling major plot points, I can say the narrative surprised me several times. Some moments are genuinely heartbreaking, while others create hope in a world that often feels hopeless. The pacing is also excellent. The game knows when to slow down and let players absorb the atmosphere before throwing them into terrifying or action-heavy sequences again.

One of my favorite aspects of the game is how immersive it feels mechanically. Your gas mask can crack during combat. Your watch helps track filter time and stealth visibility. You manually wipe dirt and blood from your mask. Your lighter helps navigate darkness and detect dangerous air currents. These mechanics might sound small individually, but together they make you feel physically present in the world.

The moral system is another feature I appreciate because it avoids feeling overly obvious. Instead of simply labeling actions as “good” or “evil,” the game quietly observes your behavior. Exploration, compassion, listening to conversations, avoiding unnecessary violence, and helping others can influence outcomes. This system encourages players to think about their actions rather than treating every situation as target practice.

What impressed me most is that the game respects the player’s intelligence. It does not constantly interrupt immersion with excessive tutorials or endless objective markers. You are expected to pay attention to the environment, listen to dialogue, and think carefully. That design philosophy helps make the experience feel more mature and immersive compared to many modern shooters.

Another thing worth praising is the pacing variety. The game is not nonstop action from beginning to end. Some sections focus on horror, others on stealth, exploration, survival, storytelling, or large-scale combat. Because of this variety, the experience rarely becomes repetitive. Every chapter introduces new situations, environments, or emotional tones that keep the journey engaging.

The outdoor sections are especially memorable. Exploring the ruins of Moscow above ground creates a completely different atmosphere from the claustrophobic tunnels. The destroyed city covered in snow and radiation looks hauntingly beautiful. These moments often feel lonely and reflective, reminding players how much humanity has lost.

This game is a 10/10 for me

It's a fun game, but as sequels go, its not quite as good as 2033. Could have done without the pole dancers and all that ♥♥♥♥. And you get the "bad" ending from killing the commie traitors. but besides all that its a really fun game.

Last Light feels much more cinematic and action-focused compared to Metro 2033. The large-scale war between stations sometimes makes it feel closer to Call of Duty than survival horror. Metro 2033 had a much heavier atmosphere where you constantly felt weak, isolated, and low on resources.

That said, Last Light is still a great game. Gunplay feels better, the world is bigger, and some surface moments are incredible.
The best game of the Metro series, great gameplay and atmosphere, high replayability, had a lot of fun getting 100% on this one.

Replaying the Metro games after years. I rated the first already and it was exactly as good as I remembered it being.
Straight to the point, I'm giving this game a 7 out of 10. Being above average/good but not as good as Metro 2033 (Redux).

Being very similar to the first, the game was very immersive and pulled me right in. The atmosphere was on point again too. I enjoyed the levels above ground in Last Light more than the ones in 2033.
Speaking of levels, I overall enjoyed the general level design less. It encouraged head on fighting more than stealth in my experience, so not a huge fan.

The story was interesting and felt like a natural continuation of the first game and book. Though, the pacing was a bit weird towards the end and the ending, while satisfying in my opinion felt abrupt. It might've been because I got the bad ending though. I did watch the canon, good ending on youtube and it certainly felt a bit more natural overall.
I did kind of mention this when writing about the level design above, I didn't like how Artyom felt more like an "action hero" this time. Though, it could also be his sensible, natural evolution in the overarching story.

For graphics/performance there's not much to say. It looked good and ran very well. I played on Linux and had no issues.

And for gameplay, there was overall less things to explore in my experience. I did try and go out of my way to get sidetracked/find eastereggs/secrets and there either wasn't as much as in the first game or they were less impactful for me. As mentioned multiple times earlier, the game was very action heavy compared to the first one. By that I mean that combat felt often like the better choice compared to stealth. I did make it a point to try to stealth through as much of the game as possible.
For gunplay/guns overall there isn't much to add. Like in the first game, the guns felt good and the customizability was nice. There was also a bit more variety compared to the first game, which was nice.

The game also felt easier and less scary compared to the first one. I played on the "default" difficulty (Survival, normal) which I believe is the recommended for the "first" playthrough, though this technically was my second. Maybe I'll make more expansive reviews in the future after I've played them on Ranger hardcore.
Gameplay much improved from Metro 2033 and a very compelling story in a captivating universe. Fantastic game.
